---
title: "CS 203: Software Tools and Techniques for AI"
subtitle: "Lecture Slides & Lab Exercises"
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Course Information

**Instructor:** Prof. Nipun Batra, IIT Gandhinagar

**Semester:** January 2026

**Format:** 1.5-hour lecture + 3-hour hands-on lab each week

**Course Website:** [nipunbatra.github.io/stt-ai-26](https://nipunbatra.github.io/stt-ai-26/)

**Repository:** [github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching)
:::

## About This Course

This 15-week course covers the **complete software engineering stack for modern AI development**. While most ML courses focus on algorithms, this course focuses on the engineering practices that make ML systems work in the real world.

### Learning Outcomes

By the end of this course, you will:

1. **Build end-to-end ML systems** — From data collection to production deployment
2. **Choose the right tools** — Know which tool to use for any data/ML task
3. **Debug systematically** — Understand why things break and fix them
4. **Write production code** — Code that others can understand, maintain, and reproduce

### What You'll Learn

| Module | Key Skills |
|--------|------------|
| **Data Engineering** | APIs, web scraping, data validation, annotation pipelines |
| **Labeling at Scale** | Active learning, weak supervision, LLM-based labeling |
| **Model Development** | LLM APIs, AutoML, transfer learning, fine-tuning |
| **Engineering** | Git, reproducibility, experiment tracking, CI/CD |
| **Deployment** | FastAPI, Gradio, Streamlit, Docker |
| **Optimization** | Profiling, quantization, ONNX, pruning |

---

## Quick Navigation

**Part I — Data Engineering (Weeks 1-5)**

| Week | Topic | Lecture | Lab |
|:----:|-------|:-------:|:---:|
| 1 | [Data Collection](#week-1-data-collection) | [PDF](pdf/week01-data-collection-lecture.pdf) ∙ [HTML](html/week01-data-collection-lecture.html) | [Notebook](labs/week01-data-collection-lab.html) |
| 2 | [Data Validation](#week-2-data-validation--quality) | [PDF](pdf/week02-data-validation-lecture.pdf) ∙ [HTML](html/week02-data-validation-lecture.html) | [Notebook](labs/week02-data-validation-lab.html) |
| 3 | [Data Labeling](#week-3-data-labeling--annotation) | [PDF](pdf/week03-data-labeling-lecture.pdf) ∙ [HTML](html/week03-data-labeling-lecture.html) | [Notebook](labs/week03-data-labeling-lab.html) |
| 4 | [Optimizing Labeling](#week-4-optimizing-labeling) | [PDF](pdf/week04-optimizing-labeling-lecture.pdf) ∙ [HTML](html/week04-optimizing-labeling-lecture.html) | [Notebook](labs/week04-optimizing-labeling-lab.html) |
| 5 | [Data Augmentation](#week-5-data-augmentation) | [PDF](pdf/week05-data-augmentation-lecture.pdf) ∙ [HTML](html/week05-data-augmentation-lecture.html) | [Notebook](labs/week05-data-augmentation-lab.html) |

**Part II — Model Development (Weeks 6-7)**

| Week | Topic | Lecture | Lab |
|:----:|-------|:-------:|:---:|
| 6 | [LLM APIs](#week-6-llm-apis--multimodal-ai) | [PDF](pdf/week06-llm-apis-lecture.pdf) ∙ [HTML](html/week06-llm-apis-lecture.html) | [Notebook](labs/week06-llm-apis-lab.html) |
| 7 | [Model Development](#week-7-model-development--training) | [PDF](pdf/week07-model-development-lecture.pdf) ∙ [HTML](html/week07-model-development-lecture.html) | [Slides](html/week07-model-development-lab.html) |

**Part III — Engineering & Deployment (Weeks 8-13)**

| Week | Topic | Lecture | Follow-Along |
|:----:|-------|:-------:|:---:|
| 8 | [Git](#week-8-git) | [HTML](html/week08-git-lecture.html) | [Script](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/lecture-demos/week08/git_followalong.sh) ∙ [PDF](lecture-demos/week08/git_followalong.pdf) |
| 9 | [Reproducibility](#week-9-reproducibility--environments) | [HTML](html/week09-reproducibility-lecture.html) | [Script](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/lecture-demos/week09/reproducibility_followalong.sh) ∙ [PDF](lecture-demos/week09/reproducibility_followalong.pdf) |
| 10 | [Experiment Tracking](#week-10-experiment-tracking--reproducibility) | [HTML](html/week10-experiments-lecture.html) | [Notebook](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/lecture-demos/week10/experiments_notebook.ipynb) ∙ [Script](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/lecture-demos/week10/experiments_followalong.sh) |
| 11 | [Testing & CI/CD](#week-11-testing--cicd) | [HTML](html/week11-cicd-lecture.html) | [Script](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/lecture-demos/week11/cicd_followalong.sh) ∙ [PDF](lecture-demos/week11/cicd_followalong.pdf) |
| 12 | [APIs & Demos](#week-12-apis--demos) | [HTML](html/week12-apis-lecture.html) | [Script](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/lecture-demos/week12/apis_followalong.sh) ∙ [PDF](lecture-demos/week12/apis_followalong.pdf) |
| 13 | [Profiling & Quantization](#week-13-profiling--quantization) | [HTML](html/week13-profiling-lecture.html) | [Notebook](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/lecture-demos/week13/profiling_notebook.ipynb) ∙ [Script](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/lecture-demos/week13/profiling_followalong.sh) |

**Part IV — Production (Weeks 14-15)**

| Week | Topic | Lecture | Lab |
|:----:|-------|:-------:|:---:|
| 14 | [Monitoring](#week-14-model-monitoring--observability) | [PDF](pdf/week14-model-monitoring-lecture.pdf) ∙ [HTML](html/week14-model-monitoring-lecture.html) | [Slides](html/week14-model-monitoring-lab.html) |
| 15 | [Summary](#week-15-course-summary--future-trends) | [PDF](pdf/week15-course-summary-lecture.pdf) ∙ [HTML](html/week15-course-summary-lecture.html) | — |

---

## Part I: Data Engineering (Weeks 1-5)

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Learning Goal:** Transform raw, messy data into clean, labeled datasets ready for ML training.
:::

### Week 1: Data Collection

Build robust data collection pipelines using HTTP APIs and web scraping.

**Key Tools:** Chrome DevTools, curl, requests, BeautifulSoup, Playwright

| | Materials |
|--|:--|
| **Lecture** | [PDF](pdf/week01-data-collection-lecture.pdf) ∙ [HTML](html/week01-data-collection-lecture.html) ∙ [Source](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/slides/week01-data-collection-lecture.md) |
| **Lab (Notebook)** | [View](labs/week01-data-collection-lab.html) ∙ [Download .ipynb](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/raw/master/labs/week01-data-collection-lab.ipynb) |

**Topics Covered:**

- HTTP fundamentals (methods, status codes, headers)
- REST APIs and authentication
- Rate limiting and pagination
- Web scraping with BeautifulSoup
- Building a movie data collector

---

### Week 2: Data Validation & Quality

Ensure data integrity through validation schemas and cleaning pipelines.

**Key Tools:** jq, csvkit, Pydantic, pandas

| | Materials |
|--|:--|
| **Lecture** | [PDF](pdf/week02-data-validation-lecture.pdf) ∙ [HTML](html/week02-data-validation-lecture.html) ∙ [Source](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/slides/week02-data-validation-lecture.md) |
| **Lab (Notebook)** | [View](labs/week02-data-validation-lab.html) ∙ [Download .ipynb](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/raw/master/labs/week02-data-validation-lab.ipynb) |

**Topics Covered:**

- CLI data inspection (head, tail, wc, jq)
- Schema validation with JSON Schema and Pydantic
- Data profiling and quality metrics
- Handling missing values and type mismatches

---

### Week 3: Data Labeling & Annotation

Set up annotation workflows and measure inter-annotator agreement.

**Key Tools:** Label Studio, Cohen's Kappa, CVAT

| | Materials |
|--|:--|
| **Lecture** | [PDF](pdf/week03-data-labeling-lecture.pdf) ∙ [HTML](html/week03-data-labeling-lecture.html) ∙ [Source](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/slides/week03-data-labeling-lecture.md) |
| **Lab (Notebook)** | [View](labs/week03-data-labeling-lab.html) ∙ [Download .ipynb](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/raw/master/labs/week03-data-labeling-lab.ipynb) |

**Topics Covered:**

- Annotation task types (text, image, audio, video)
- Setting up Label Studio
- Inter-annotator agreement metrics (Kappa, IoU)
- Quality control and annotation guidelines

---

### Week 4: Optimizing Labeling

Reduce labeling costs using active learning, weak supervision, and LLM-based labeling.

**Key Tools:** modAL, Snorkel, cleanlab, Gemini API

| | Materials |
|--|:--|
| **Lecture** | [PDF](pdf/week04-optimizing-labeling-lecture.pdf) ∙ [HTML](html/week04-optimizing-labeling-lecture.html) ∙ [Source](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/slides/week04-optimizing-labeling-lecture.md) |
| **Lab (Notebook)** | [View](labs/week04-optimizing-labeling-lab.html) ∙ [Download .ipynb](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/raw/master/labs/week04-optimizing-labeling-lab.ipynb) |

**Topics Covered:**

- Active learning strategies (uncertainty sampling, QBC)
- Weak supervision with labeling functions (Snorkel)
- LLM-based labeling with Gemini API
- Handling noisy labels (cleanlab)

---

### Week 5: Data Augmentation

Expand training datasets through synthetic transformations.

**Key Tools:** Albumentations, nlpaug, audiomentations

| | Materials |
|--|:--|
| **Lecture** | [PDF](pdf/week05-data-augmentation-lecture.pdf) ∙ [HTML](html/week05-data-augmentation-lecture.html) ∙ [Source](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/slides/week05-data-augmentation-lecture.md) |
| **Lab (Notebook)** | [View](labs/week05-data-augmentation-lab.html) ∙ [Download .ipynb](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/raw/master/labs/week05-data-augmentation-lab.ipynb) |

**Topics Covered:**

- Image augmentation (geometric, color, noise)
- Text augmentation (synonym replacement, back-translation)
- Audio augmentation (noise, pitch, speed)
- AutoAugment and test-time augmentation

---

## Part II: Model Development (Weeks 6-7)

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Learning Goal:** Build and train ML models using APIs, AutoML, and fine-tuning.
:::

### Week 6: LLM APIs & Multimodal AI

Leverage foundation models for text, vision, and audio tasks.

**Key Tools:** Gemini API, OpenRouter, OpenAI API

| | Materials |
|--|:--|
| **Lecture** | [PDF](pdf/week06-llm-apis-lecture.pdf) ∙ [HTML](html/week06-llm-apis-lecture.html) ∙ [Source](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/slides/week06-llm-apis-lecture.md) |
| **Lab (Notebook)** | [View](labs/week06-llm-apis-lab.html) ∙ [Download .ipynb](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/raw/master/labs/week06-llm-apis-lab.ipynb) |

**Topics Covered:**

- LLM fundamentals (tokens, temperature, sampling)
- Prompt engineering (zero-shot, few-shot, chain-of-thought)
- Multimodal capabilities (vision, audio, video)
- Structured outputs and function calling

---

### Week 7: Model Development & Training

Train and evaluate machine learning models effectively.

**Key Tools:** scikit-learn, AutoGluon, PyTorch, LoRA

| | PDF | HTML | Source |
|--|:--:|:--:|:--:|
| **Lecture** | [Download](pdf/week07-model-development-lecture.pdf) | [View](html/week07-model-development-lecture.html) | [GitHub](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/slides/week07-model-development-lecture.md) |
| **Lab** | [Download](pdf/week07-model-development-lab.pdf) | [View](html/week07-model-development-lab.html) | [GitHub](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/slides/week07-model-development-lab.md) |

**Topics Covered:**

- Baseline models and cross-validation
- AutoML with AutoGluon
- Transfer learning and fine-tuning
- LLM fine-tuning with LoRA

---

## Part III: Engineering & Deployment (Weeks 8-13)

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Learning Goal:** Version code & environments, track experiments, automate with CI/CD, and ship models as APIs.
:::

### Week 8: Git

Version control fundamentals and collaborative workflows.

**Key Tools:** Git, GitHub, SSH keys

| | Materials |
|--|:--|
| **Lecture** | [HTML](html/week08-git-lecture.html) ∙ [Source](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/slides/week08-git-lecture.md) |
| **Follow-Along** | [Script](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/lecture-demos/week08/git_followalong.sh) ∙ [PDF](lecture-demos/week08/git_followalong.pdf) |

**Topics Covered:**

- Git internals (blobs, trees, commits, refs)
- Branching, merging, and rebasing
- Collaborative workflows (PRs, code review)
- .gitignore and best practices

---

### Week 9: Reproducibility & Environments

Version your environment so code runs the same everywhere.

**Key Tools:** venv, pip, conda, Docker, Docker Compose

| | Materials |
|--|:--|
| **Lecture** | [HTML](html/week09-reproducibility-lecture.html) ∙ [Source](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/slides/week09-reproducibility-lecture.md) |
| **Follow-Along** | [Script](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/lecture-demos/week09/reproducibility_followalong.sh) ∙ [PDF](lecture-demos/week09/reproducibility_followalong.pdf) |

**Topics Covered:**

- Virtual environments and `requirements.txt`
- Version pinning and dependency management
- Random seeds for reproducibility
- Docker containers and Docker Compose
- Project structure best practices

---

### Week 10: Experiment Tracking & Reproducibility

Track hyperparameters, compare runs, and ensure reproducible results.

**Key Tools:** scikit-learn, Optuna, PyTorch, W&B, MLflow

| | Materials |
|--|:--|
| **Lecture** | [HTML](html/week10-experiments-lecture.html) ∙ [Source](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/slides/week10-experiments-lecture.md) |
| **Notebook** | [View .ipynb](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/lecture-demos/week10/experiments_notebook.ipynb) |
| **Follow-Along** | [Script](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/lecture-demos/week10/experiments_followalong.sh) ∙ [PDF](lecture-demos/week10/experiments_followalong.pdf) |

**Topics Covered:**

- Grid search vs random search vs Bayesian optimization (Optuna)
- Cross-validation variants (K-Fold, Stratified, Group)
- PyTorch reproducibility (seeds, deterministic algorithms, DataLoader)
- Multi-seed reporting
- W&B experiment tracking and sweeps

---

### Week 11: Testing & CI/CD

Automate testing and deployment with CI/CD pipelines.

**Key Tools:** pytest, pre-commit, Ruff, GitHub Actions

| | Materials |
|--|:--|
| **Lecture** | [HTML](html/week11-cicd-lecture.html) ∙ [Source](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/slides/week11-cicd-lecture.md) |
| **Follow-Along** | [Script](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/lecture-demos/week11/cicd_followalong.sh) ∙ [PDF](lecture-demos/week11/cicd_followalong.pdf) |

**Topics Covered:**

- Testing pyramid (unit, integration, end-to-end)
- pytest (fixtures, parametrize, coverage)
- Pre-commit hooks and Ruff linting
- GitHub Actions (workflows, matrix builds, caching, artifacts)
- Branch protection and PR workflows

---

## Part IV: Shipping & Optimization (Weeks 12-15)

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Learning Goal:** Ship models as APIs/demos and optimize for production.
:::

### Week 12: APIs & Demos

Serve ML models as APIs and build interactive demos.

**Key Tools:** FastAPI, Pydantic, Gradio, Streamlit

| | Materials |
|--|:--|
| **Lecture** | [HTML](html/week12-apis-lecture.html) ∙ [Source](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/slides/week12-apis-lecture.md) |
| **Follow-Along** | [Script](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/lecture-demos/week12/apis_followalong.sh) ∙ [PDF](lecture-demos/week12/apis_followalong.pdf) |

**Topics Covered:**

- REST API fundamentals (HTTP methods, status codes)
- FastAPI with Pydantic validation
- Model serialization (joblib, torch.save, ONNX)
- Gradio and Streamlit for interactive demos
- Dockerizing ML services

---

### Week 13: Profiling & Quantization

Profile performance bottlenecks and optimize model size/speed.

**Key Tools:** torch.profiler, cProfile, ONNX Runtime, quantization

| | Materials |
|--|:--|
| **Lecture** | [HTML](html/week13-profiling-lecture.html) ∙ [Source](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/slides/week13-profiling-lecture.md) |
| **Notebook** | [View .ipynb](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/lecture-demos/week13/profiling_notebook.ipynb) |
| **Follow-Along** | [Script](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/lecture-demos/week13/profiling_followalong.sh) ∙ [PDF](lecture-demos/week13/profiling_followalong.pdf) |

**Topics Covered:**

- Number formats (FP32, FP16, BF16, INT8)
- Quantization (dynamic, static PTQ, QAT)
- ONNX export and runtime optimization
- Profiling with torch.profiler and cProfile
- Pruning and knowledge distillation

---

### Week 14: Model Monitoring & Observability

Detect and respond to model degradation in production.

**Key Tools:** Evidently AI, Prometheus, Data drift detection

| | PDF | HTML | Source |
|--|:--:|:--:|:--:|
| **Lecture** | [Download](pdf/week14-model-monitoring-lecture.pdf) | [View](html/week14-model-monitoring-lecture.html) | [GitHub](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/slides/week14-model-monitoring-lecture.md) |
| **Lab** | [Download](pdf/week14-model-monitoring-lab.pdf) | [View](html/week14-model-monitoring-lab.html) | [GitHub](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/slides/week14-model-monitoring-lab.md) |

**Topics Covered:**

- Data drift vs concept drift
- Statistical tests for drift detection
- Monitoring dashboards
- Alerting and retraining triggers

---

### Week 15: Course Summary & Future Trends

Review the ML pipeline and explore emerging topics.

**Key Topics:** LLMOps, AI Agents, Edge AI, Final Projects

| | PDF | HTML | Source |
|--|:--:|:--:|:--:|
| **Lecture** | [Download](pdf/week15-course-summary-lecture.pdf) | [View](html/week15-course-summary-lecture.html) | [GitHub](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/slides/week15-course-summary-lecture.md) |

**Topics Covered:**

- Complete ML pipeline review
- Emerging trends (LLMOps, AI Agents)
- Career paths in ML engineering
- Final project presentations

---

## Additional Resources

::: {.callout-tip}
## Useful Links
- **Course Syllabus:** [View on main website](https://nipunbatra.github.io/stt-ai-26/)
- **Course Specification:** [COURSE_SPEC.md](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/COURSE_SPEC.md) - pedagogical philosophy and content guidelines
- **Build Instructions:** See [README.md](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/blob/master/README.md) for building slides locally
- **Report Issues:** [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/issues)
- **Diagram Generators:** [diagram-generators/](https://github.com/nipunbatra/stt-ai-teaching/tree/master/diagram-generators) - Python scripts for all course diagrams
:::

### Core Tools Covered

| Category | Tools |
|----------|-------|
| **Data Collection** | requests, BeautifulSoup, Playwright |
| **Data Quality** | Pydantic, pandas, jq |
| **Labeling** | Label Studio, modAL, Snorkel |
| **LLM APIs** | Gemini API, OpenAI API |
| **Model Training** | scikit-learn, AutoGluon, PyTorch |
| **Version Control** | Git, GitHub, Docker |
| **Experiment Tracking** | Optuna, W&B, MLflow |
| **Testing & CI** | pytest, pre-commit, Ruff, GitHub Actions |
| **Deployment** | FastAPI, Gradio, Streamlit |
| **Optimization** | torch.profiler, ONNX Runtime, quantization |

---

<div style="text-align: center; margin-top: 2em; padding: 1em; background-color: #f8f9fa; border-radius: 8px;">
  <p><strong>Course Materials by Prof. Nipun Batra</strong> | IIT Gandhinagar</p>
  <p style="font-size: 0.9em;">Last updated: March 2026</p>
</div>
